quote_of_day.text:
>>59836
>women tend to be more selective, but at the same time they have lot of options
Only woman who showed clear interest in me was sub5 and overweight. Hypergamy in Bangalore is pretty brutal. I’m 5’8, lightish skin and 6/10 facially
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A high pressure system of objectification moved over the board this morning, bringing with it a torrent of comments on a woman's physique. The discussion centered around her ethnic features and body shape, drawing a flurry of responses.

A scattered front of anime-related discourse developed later, marked by personal attacks and derisive comments. This gave way to a low-pressure system of esoteric discussion, as one anon invoked Ozymandias on the topics of namefagging and remote viewing.

Meanwhile, a gentle drizzle of personal updates touched down, with an anon sharing news of their sister's admission to Delhi University's North campus.

A brief cold snap of concern also passed through, as another anon raised the issue of energy drink consumption and cardiac risks.

articles.title: Anon Praises Woman's Physique, Another Anon Discusses Ethnic Features
articles.body:
Anon started a thread praising a woman's physical appearance, describing her as having a perfect hourglass figure. The post mentioned her waist and physique, suggesting she was 'made for BDL'.

Another anon responded, discussing the physical characteristics of Sindhi women, attributing their features to a mix of Aryan and Dravidian ancestry.

This anon claimed Sindhi women have facial features, skin tone, and bone structure from Aryans, and hair, thickness, and body shape from Dravidians. No further discussion was provided in the given posts.

articles.title: Anime discussion devolves into personal attacks
articles.body:
Anon started the discussion with a post saying ">this is what animegoys watch in current year".

One respondent, Anon, mentioned they were watching an anime episode and found it comfortable and nostalgic, recalling moments of being distracted by a female character's assets as a horny teenager.

Another Anon jokingly mentioned that people take themselves too seriously. The conversation took a turn when Ozymandias got involved, making claims about degeneracy and social conformity in Japan.

Ozymandias also denied being a certain user and clarified that a UN flag was their default account flag.

The discussion further deteriorated into personal attacks, with Anon accusing Ozymandias of being a "Dravidian subhuman abbo" and Ozymandias challenging others to post pictures or timestamps as proof.

The thread also included various insults and denials, with one Anon stating they deleted a side profile picture of Ozymandias. Ozymandias maintained they had never posted their face online and swore on god.

articles.title: Ozymandias on Namefagging and Remote Viewing
articles.body:
Anon claimed Ozymandias, also known as "ozy", was humiliated after attempting remote viewing on bharatchan. Ozy allegedly made incorrect guesses about physical characteristics.

Another anon stated they witnessed the exchange and recalled ozy getting brutally humiliated. The discussion turned to ozy's past behavior, with one poster mentioning ozy had used a tripcode.

Ozymandias responded, stating they never used a tripcode. Ozy mentioned that a lot of bharatchan is archived. Anon joked that no one saves ozy's posts.

The conversation shifted to namefagging, with Ozymandias arguing that it adds a forum-like quality to the imageboard, promoting individualism and better discussions. Anon dismissed ozy as a wannabe micro-celebrity trying to cling to relevance.

Anon also accused ozy of having a humiliation fetish and being attention-starved. The topic of ozy's identity and past actions continued to be debated among posters.

articles.title: Anon shares sister's DU admission
articles.body:
Anon posted that their sister got into DU North campus in the first round of counselling.

Another anon responded with an unrelated comment about someone being delusional about their caste and skin color.

A different conversation emerged with an anon discussing hypergamy in Bangalore, saying women there are selective but have many options.

One anon noted that attractiveness plays a role in these dynamics, stating that an attractive person would likely take the chance to have sex with attractive girls.

The conversation also touched on Symbiosis in Pune, with someone asking what students there normally do.

articles.title: Energy drink consumption and cardiac risks
articles.body:
Anon posted a string of b's.

Anon shared cases of young men dying from cardiac arrest due to habitual energy drink consumption, citing a 19-year-old California man who died after consuming two 16-ounce cans of Monster Energy daily for three years, and a 21-year-old man who died from cardiac arrhythmia.

Another anon mentioned they had built a tolerance to caffeine from daily coffee consumption, finding a 500ml can of Monster had little effect.

Ozymandias noted that while 200mg of caffeine may not be lethal for most, it could be problematic for those with pre-existing heart conditions.
